title: RTC ICA FY2020 through FY2026
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 22, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Khalil Wilson, Assistant Public Works Director
DEPT: Public Works and City Manager's Office
SUBJECT: Annual update on Projects listed in the Regional Transportation Commission Interlocal Cooperative Agreements for Fiscal Years 2020 through 2026

This memo provides Council with an update on the current work phase and anticipated construction schedule for projects approved through Interlocal Cooperative Agreements (ICA) with the Regional Transportation Commission (RTC) for Fiscal Years 2020 to 2026. This memo highlights the projects in various design phases, and the full list of all projects is included in the attachment.

The Regional Transportation Commission's Regional Transportation Plan (RTP) is a 20-year guiding document that includes short-term and long-term strategies to foster development of an integrated multi-modal regional transportation system that facilitates the safe and efficient movement of people and goods. Next, the Regional Transportation Commission's 5-year Regional Transportation Improvement Plan (RTIP) is developed from the short-term list of projects and programs, and then Interlocal Cooperative Agreements are developed.

Since the Regional Transportation Commission does not own any roads, in accordance with state law, an Interlocal Cooperative Agreement is required to outline the responsibilities for delivering new projects within the City's jurisdiction and to authorize the Regional Transportation Commission to exercise eminent domain if necessary.

These Interlocal Cooperative Agreements are essential for advancing the tasks outlined in these programs. The Regional Transportation Commission drafts separate agreements for each jurisdiction, based on the project's location. This approach provides clarity to the overarching project program and facilitates the independent approval of each Interlocal Cooperative Agreement and ensuring that delays in one jurisdiction does not impede the approval process in another.

Below is a list of projects identified in previously Council approved Interlocal Cooperative Agreements that are in various design phases of work. These projects are listed in order of the expected year when construction is scheduled to begin. All other major projects on the attachment have been completed.

fields.department: Public Works and City Manager's Office
fields.authors: Khalil Wilson, Assistant Public Works Director
fields.tldr: This memo provides the Mayor and City Council with an annual update on projects funded through Interlocal Cooperative Agreements (ICAs) with the Regional Transportation Commission (RTC) for Fiscal Years 2020–2026. Projects span preventative maintenance, roadway reconstruction, active transportation, traffic signals, bus rapid transit, bridge replacements, and capacity improvements. Many projects are in final design with construction expected in 2026, while others are in preliminary design targeting 2027–2028. ICAs are legally required because the RTC does not own roads and must partner with the City to deliver projects within its jurisdiction.
fields.key_stats: ICA estimated costs include: 2026 Preventative Maintenance $6,500,000; 2026 Corrective Maintenance $2,000,000; 2026 Roadway Reconstruction $14,000,000; 2026 Traffic Signals and Intersection Improvements $6,000,000; 2026 ITS/Traffic Management $4,000,000; 2026 Active Transportation $5,000,000; Boomtown Garson Rd Interchange Improvements $2,000,000; University Area Roadway Improvements Phase 1 $3,500,000. Projects span FY 2020 through FY 2026 ICAs. Construction timelines range from 2025 to 2028, with one project (Mt. Rose Highway Operational and Safety Improvements) currently unfunded with a TBD construction date.
fields.key_decisions: No explicit Council decisions are requested in this memo; it is an informational annual update on previously Council-approved ICAs. Council previously approved ICAs for FY 2020 through FY 2026 authorizing the RTC to deliver projects within the City's jurisdiction, including the authority to exercise eminent domain if necessary.
fields.action_items: No specific action items are identified in this memo. The memo is for informational purposes only, providing Council with a status update on active ICA projects.
